text.text: Finally got to try Molly Tea, and it is obvious why they are the top boba tea place. First off the interior is huge with a great aesthetic, and the gigantic tea cup outside is iconic. You order on these huge tablets and get a text when your order is ready. The quality of the tea is evident, and the tapioca is honestly the best I have ever had. Perfectly chewy and at the perfect sweetness level. They have plenty of merch as well and the staff were friendly.

originalText.text: Finally got to try Molly Tea, and it is obvious why they are the top boba tea place. First off the interior is huge with a great aesthetic, and the gigantic tea cup outside is iconic. You order on these huge tablets and get a text when your order is ready. The quality of the tea is evident, and the tapioca is honestly the best I have ever had. Perfectly chewy and at the perfect sweetness level. They have plenty of merch as well and the staff were friendly.

text.text:
Tea is good but definitely not worth the wait. Took us almost 45 minutes to get our order.

We tried a matcha jasmine tea and a mango jasmine smoothie. The match jasmine tea is very good. The flavors of both matcha and jasmine blend well and doesn’t overpower each other. The mango jasmine smoothie is decent, but nothing to write home about.

I don’t get the hype TBH. If you must go, order way ahead and get there in time to pick up.

text.text:
I would say this was an average spot - it's like a typical boba place, wouldn't say you need to go out of your way for it but if you're near it, pretty decent choices and place. There's plenty of space to sit and parking in the front and back and street parking but gets filled up pretty quickly.
I wanted to try this place because I heard from a friend that it was sooo good- compared to regular boba places, I would say this is a little stronger in the tea but otherwise it's about the same.
I got the premium matcha jasmine tea, but curious to come back to try the champaca and pistachio ones.

text.text:
Good light floral teas. I tried my Camelia Zest tea, and it was delicious, but not worth $6.79. Unfortunate how much drink volume is taken up by the fresh orange slices inside. Oranges took up even more space than ice, so that’s robbing you of more drink.

My mom tried the Snowy Gardenia $7.79 and she enjoyed it. She did not have to do the work of coming here and ordering, though. I'll take her word for it.

I ordered at 11:08pm and my number was 1213- my heart dropped when I looked at the monitor and the “ready” orders were barely in the 1100s. Which is crazy when you consider there's no drink limit per order. May as well have been a 1,000 drinks ahead of me. I received my drinks an hour later at 12:12am. I ordered my drinks on Sunday and got them on Monday!!

Even if the tea is good (it is!) no tea shop deserves above 3 stars if it takes over an hour to get drinks out. Nice or clean the shop aesthetics don't make up for poor service. Looking at other reviews here, some people had to wait 2 hours plus!

Maybe it's worth revisiting at some point and see if they act on any of their metrics and make improvements to the operation. There are clearly some bottlenecks on drink output. You can always take steps to improve like less drink options overall, limit options during rush hours or late at night, set a maximum # of items per order, hire more people, complete orders in batches, etc. It just goes on and on! Something (anything!) to improve the wait time.

The parking lot is super small but there's street parking around residential and other businesses, since this is off Valley Blvd. Some indoor and outdoor seating but also not enough for how many people are coming in and out from online orders and in-person ordering tea.

text.text:
Luckily, it wasn't busy, but there were a lot
Numbers being prepared,
I asked the staff about the wait time in case it was long; surprisingly, she said it was less than 10 minutes.
Ordered at the kiosk, ordered the premium Jasmine milk tea with oat milk, and 70% sweetness
came out quickly. The milk tea was fragrant and delicious.
